steps.title: Apply the vertical shift rule
steps.content:
When a function is shifted up by $k$ units, we add $k$ to the function value. For a shift up by 9 units, we have:
$$g(x) = f(x) + 9$$

steps.title: Check all choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$g(x) = 3x + 5 + 9$': Equals $3x + 14$, which is $f(x)$ shifted up by 9 ✓
  • Choice B — '$g(x) = 3x + 5 - 9$': Equals $3x - 4$, a shift down by 9, not up ✗
  • Choice C — '$g(x) = 3x + 45$': Adds 9 inside the argument instead of to the output; also $5 \times 9 = 45$ confuses multiplication with addition ✗
  • Choice D — '$g(x) = 27x + 5$': Multiplies the slope by 9 instead of adding 9 to the output ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
